title: English for Everyday Life — Quick Reference
topics: emergencies · phone · post office · bank · pharmacy
description: Praktická angličtina pro každodenní situace — pošta, banka, lékárna, tísňová volání.

vocabulary.en: I'd like to send this parcel.
I need something for a headache.
Is this available without a prescription?

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.text: Excuse me, I think I've been pickpocketed. My wallet is gone.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.text: I'm sorry to hear that. You should report it to the police immediately.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.text: Where is the nearest police station?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.text: It's about 200 metres down this street, on the right.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.text: Thank you. Should I also call my bank to block my cards?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.text: Yes, definitely — do that right away.

examples.en: Could you speak more slowly, please? I'm still learning English.
examples.cz: Mohl/a byste prosím mluvit pomaleji? Stále se učím anglicky.

examples.en: I'd like to send this to Prague by registered post.
examples.cz: Chtěl/a bych toto poslat do Prahy doporučeně.

examples.en: Is this medication available without a prescription?
examples.cz: Je tento lék dostupný bez receptu?

examples.en: Hold the line — I'll transfer you to the right department.
examples.cz: Zůstaňte na lince — přepojím vás na příslušné oddělení.

quiz.sentence: Could you ___ that, please? I didn't catch it. (repeat)
I'd like to ___ £100 from my account. (take out)
Please ___ the line — I'll connect you to the manager.
I've ___ my passport. What should I do? (lost)

tasks: 1. Role-play: you've lost your bag abroad — practise the conversation at a police station
2. Write the key phrases you'd need in a pharmacy in English
3. Practise calling a bank in English — what would you say to report a lost card?
